name: Streak System with Visual Feedback
vision: Add a daily streak counter (≥3 prompts/day) computed from history.jsonl, displayed in the stats tab and as a notch badge, with best-streak tracking and real-time updates.

tasks.title: Add streak computation to HistoryStatsLoader
tasks.description:
Modify `periquito-app/periquito/Services/HistoryStatsLoader.swift`. Add `currentStreak: Int`, `bestStreak: Int`, and `todayCount: Int` fields to `HistoryStats`. In the `load()` function, alongside existing parsing, also extract the calendar date from each entry's ISO8601 `date` field using `Calendar.current`. Build a dictionary of `[DateString: Int]` counting evaluated prompts per day (good + correction only). A 'qualifying day' has ≥3 prompts. After parsing, compute:

1. `todayCount` — number of evaluated prompts with today's date.
2. `currentStreak` — walk backwards from today: if today qualifies (≥3), include it and check yesterday, etc. If today has <3, start checking from yesterday (streak not broken until day ends with <3). Count consecutive qualifying days.
3. `bestStreak` — iterate all dates sorted ascending, find the longest consecutive qualifying run.

Use `Calendar.current.startOfDay(for:)` for date grouping. Update the `empty` static to include zeroed streak fields.

tests.command: xcodebuild -project periquito-app/periquito.xcodeproj -scheme periquito -configuration Debug build 2>&1 | tail -5

acceptance: HistoryStats has currentStreak, bestStreak, and todayCount fields
Qualifying day requires ≥3 evaluated prompts
Current streak counts consecutive qualifying days touching today or yesterday
Today with <3 prompts does not break streak (checks from yesterday)
Best streak is the longest qualifying run in all history
todayCount reflects prompts analyzed today
HistoryStats.empty includes zeroed streak fields
Build succeeds

hints: Parse ISO8601 date strings to get calendar day — use ISO8601DateFormatter then Calendar.current.startOfDay()
Dictionary key can be a formatted date string like yyyy-MM-dd for grouping
For best streak: sort unique qualifying dates, walk forward counting consecutive days
All streak fields are Int, not optional — 0 means no streak

tasks.title: Add streak section to StatsView
tasks.description:
Modify `periquito-app/periquito/Views/StatsView.swift`. Add a streak section between the level row and the accuracy hero. Layout:

1. A row with two stat cards side by side:
   - Left card: '🔥 N' (current streak in days) with label 'Current Streak'. Use TerminalColors.amber for the number.
   - Right card: '⭐ N' (best streak in days) with label 'Best Streak'. Use TerminalColors.dimmedText for the number.
2. If currentStreak >= bestStreak AND currentStreak > 0, show 'New best!' text in TerminalColors.green below the cards.
3. Below the cards, show today's progress toward qualifying: 'M/3 today' in dimmed text. If todayCount >= 3, show '✓ Today counts!' in green instead.
4. When streak is 0, show '0 days' (not hidden).

Reuse the existing `statCard` helper pattern. Read streak data from the `stats` parameter (HistoryStats).

tests.command: xcodebuild -project periquito-app/periquito.xcodeproj -scheme periquito -configuration Debug build 2>&1 | tail -5

acceptance: Current streak shown with fire emoji and amber color
Best streak shown with star emoji and dimmed color
'New best!' shown when current >= best and > 0
Today's progress shown as 'M/3 today' or '✓ Today counts!'
0-day streak displays cleanly as '0'
Section placed between level row and accuracy hero
Build succeeds

hints: Reuse the statCard pattern already in StatsView
Use HStack(spacing: 12) for the two streak cards, same as the existing stat cards row

tasks.title: Add streak fire badge to GrassIslandView sprites
tasks.description:
Modify `periquito-app/periquito/Views/GrassIslandView.swift`. In `GrassSpriteView`, add a second overlay badge at `alignment: .bottomLeading` (the level badge is already at `.bottomTrailing`). Show '🔥N' where N is the current streak from `HistoryStats`. Only display when streak ≥ 1. Use 10pt font, with black shadow for contrast against the notch background. Offset with `offset(x: -4, y: 4)` to mirror the level badge position.

Since `GrassSpriteView` doesn't have access to stats, you'll need to read the streak from a shared source. The simplest approach: add a `@State` streak property to `GrassIslandView` that loads on appear and refreshes via `onChange(of:)` on the session store. Or, add `currentStreak` as a stored property on `LevelManager` that gets updated when stats are loaded. Choose the approach that requires least plumbing.

tests.command: xcodebuild -project periquito-app/periquito.xcodeproj -scheme periquito -configuration Debug build 2>&1 | tail -5

acceptance: '🔥N' badge visible on sprites when streak ≥ 1
Badge hidden when streak is 0
Badge positioned at bottom-left (opposite from level badge at bottom-right)
Uses 10pt font with black shadow for contrast
Badge visible on both active session sprites and idle sprite
No visual conflict with the existing level emoji badge
Build succeeds

hints: Level badge is at .bottomTrailing with offset(x: 4, y: 4) — mirror to .bottomLeading with offset(x: -4, y: 4)
Simplest approach: add var currentStreak: Int to LevelManager, update it from ExpandedPanelView when stats load
GrassSpriteView is private — add the overlay inside it, reading from LevelManager.shared.currentStreak
